acceptance_criteria: The PackFactory root has schema-valid objective, backlog, and work-state files.
Root memory refresh and root startup guidance point at those files as the canonical PackFactory work tracker.
completion_signals: contracts/project-objective.json, tasks/active-backlog.json, and status/work-state.json exist at the factory root.
tools/refresh_factory_autonomy_memory.py and the root docs acknowledge the root tracker.
escalation_conditions: The shared control-plane schemas prove too build-pack-specific to represent PackFactory root work cleanly.
tasks.summary: Seed the PackFactory root objective, backlog, and work-state files and wire root tooling to use them.

acceptance_criteria: The operator dashboard surfaces portfolio state, environment assignments, recent factory motion, and root task guidance clearly enough to replace long routine startup briefs.
The dashboard remains local-first and keeps the Python snapshot generator as the truth-preserving data-preparation layer.
completion_signals: The dashboard specs and implementation path align on the root executive-summary surface.
Routine PackFactory status checks no longer need a long chat-only reconstruction path.
escalation_conditions: A dashboard change would weaken the canonical registry-first truth model or create conflicting operator views.
tasks.summary: Advance the top-priority operator dashboard so it becomes the fast normal briefing surface for PackFactory state.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory can declare whether a build-pack is local-factory-managed or remote-managed without leaving dual-primary ambiguity.
Startup surfaces and operator workflows make the chosen source-of-truth mode visible.
blocked_by: Postponed by operator on 2026-03-26T22:27:19Z until PackFactory chooses to resume source-of-truth mode work.
completion_signals: A bounded source-of-truth model is documented and represented in PackFactory surfaces.
The ADF drift/reconcile problem has a clear PackFactory-native handling path.
escalation_conditions: The proposed mode model cannot stay fail-closed across local state, remote-managed state, and import/reconcile workflows.
tasks.summary: Define and adopt the build-pack source-of-truth mode so local and remote-managed lines do not behave like competing primaries.

acceptance_criteria: The Astro dashboard path has a concrete install, build, and publish plan that keeps the Python snapshot generator canonical.
Operators have a bounded wrapper path for local dev-server and publish usage.
completion_signals: The Astro dashboard upgrade spec is concrete enough to implement without improvised environment wiring.
The Astro wrapper path stays clearly downstream of the Python snapshot generator.
escalation_conditions: The Astro presentation layer would require replacing the canonical snapshot contract or adding a live backend.
tasks.summary: Turn the Astro dashboard path into a concrete install and build plan that cleanly consumes the canonical Python snapshot output.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has a reusable personality-template model that stays optional and does not collapse template reuse.
Personality inheritance is explicit during template creation and build-pack materialization.
completion_signals: A bounded personality-template design exists with clear storage and selection surfaces.
One source template can still produce multiple build-packs with different personalities.
escalation_conditions: A proposed personality system would hard-link identity to a source template and reduce template reuse.
tasks.summary: Design the optional agent-personality template system as a reusable overlay rather than a one-template identity lock-in.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has a reusable role/domain-template overlay model that stays optional and composes cleanly with personality overlays.
Role/domain inheritance is explicit during template creation and build-pack materialization without collapsing operator-specific runtime state.
completion_signals: A bounded role/domain overlay design exists with clear catalog, selection, and manifest surfaces.
PackFactory tooling can inherit, override, or clear the role/domain overlay the same way it already can for personality.
escalation_conditions: A proposed role/domain system would blur into personality so heavily that the two overlays stop being distinguishable.
A proposed role/domain system would imply literal credentials or collapse template reuse into identity lock-in.
tasks.summary: Design and implement the optional role/domain-template overlay system as a reusable framing layer that composes with personality templates.

acceptance_criteria: Root and template instruction surfaces have a clearer scan order and lower repetition without losing critical policy content.
The redesign is grounded in instruction-retention and startup-performance goals rather than prose polish alone.
completion_signals: PackFactory has a bounded plan for instruction-surface optimization tied to real startup and compliance needs.
The dashboard work and instruction-performance work share a coherent reduction path for root startup overhead.
escalation_conditions: A proposed simplification would remove or weaken critical workflow rules such as the managed remote-session control plane.
tasks.summary: Review and optimize PackFactory instruction surfaces for scan speed, retention, and startup compliance.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has an explicit implemented local-scratch-root design for transient remote-autonomy payloads that can place scratch on another directory or partition instead of requiring those writes to stay under the repo root.

The implemented workflow follows `docs/specs/project-pack-factory/PROJECT-PACK-FACTORY-TRANSIENT-LOCAL-SCRATCH-ROOT-AND-STAGING-LIFECYCLE-TECH-SPEC.md`, including the scratch-versus-canonical evidence boundary, validator changes, and lifecycle guardrails.
completion_signals: The explicit scratch-root tech spec records the 2026-03-29 disk-pressure incident, the hard-wired code paths, and the required redirect design.
Operators have a documented PackFactory-supported path for placing transient remote-autonomy local writes on managed scratch storage instead of the slash-backed repo root.
Cleanup, retention, or preflight disk-pressure checks exist as bounded lifecycle guardrails on top of the new scratch-root boundary.
escalation_conditions: A proposed cleanup rule would delete canonical runtime evidence, active remote-session staging needed by an in-flight run, or other control-plane truth surfaces.
tasks.summary: Implement the explicit local-scratch-root and staging-lifecycle design in `docs/specs/project-pack-factory/PROJECT-PACK-FACTORY-TRANSIENT-LOCAL-SCRATCH-ROOT-AND-STAGING-LIFECYCLE-TECH-SPEC.md`: move transient remote-autonomy local writes behind an agent-managed scratch root that can live on another directory or partition, persist that host-local selection across agent sessions, then add cleanup, retention, and disk-pressure guards on top.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has an explicit spec for recording the Codex personal assistant template's reusable grounded-relationship-learning bundle through autonomy-improvement promotion and template-lineage memory.
The task makes clear that this is template-lineage capture work for a proved assistant behavior family, not a request to promote assistant-specific behavior into all factory defaults.
completion_signals: A factory-root spec names the reusable assistant improvement bundle, the proof artifacts, and the correct adopted-versus-pending surfaces for promotion recording.
The bounded lineage-capture path uses `record_autonomy_improvement_promotion.py` plus `refresh_template_lineage_memory.py` against `codex-personal-assistant-template-pack`.
escalation_conditions: The available assistant evidence proves only runtime-local behavior and is not strong enough to justify a template-lineage claim.
The proposed capture would blur assistant-template lineage with factory-global defaults.
tasks.summary: Record the reusable grounded relationship-learning bundle from the Codex personal assistant line through autonomy-improvement promotion and template-lineage memory.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has an explicit runtime-to-template parity spec that says when a reusable runtime slice needs a template backport record, what parity statuses are valid, and what evidence is required.
The parity workflow stays bounded to reusable behavior and does not turn into automatic runtime-to-template file syncing.
completion_signals: A factory-root parity spec exists and is linked from the root planner task.
The spec defines a machine-readable parity artifact or equivalent bounded record that can distinguish runtime-only, template-backported, and lineage-refreshed states.
escalation_conditions: The parity proposal would imply broad automatic backporting or weaken the separation between template source and runtime derivatives.
The evidence is too thin to justify a reusable parity workflow beyond one-off assistant handling.
tasks.summary: Define a bounded PackFactory parity and backport workflow for reusable runtime behavior that should be reflected in a source template.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has an explicit root-level tech spec for a shared headless-browser proof tool that validates real browser behavior on operator-facing preview surfaces without turning that capability into a build-pack.
The spec keeps the tool bounded, aligned with the factory testing policy, and explicit that the first usage is on-demand proof rather than a broad default gate.
The spec names at least one concrete proving-ground case, such as the ADF field-manual step-expansion behavior.
completion_signals: A factory-root browser-proof spec exists under `docs/specs/project-pack-factory/` and is tied to one named root planner task.
The spec clearly distinguishes shared PackFactory tool runtime from build-pack content or build-pack-local helpers.
The next implementation pass can build a wrapper tool without re-arguing whether the capability belongs at the factory root.
escalation_conditions: The proposed tool would effectively become a broad new default UI test suite rather than a bounded proof surface.
The browser runtime model cannot be kept outside canonical build-pack truth surfaces or would create persistent tool-state ambiguity.
tasks.summary: Design a shared PackFactory headless-browser proof tool so agents can validate real operator-facing browser behavior on preview surfaces without turning that capability into a build-pack or a broad default UI test suite.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has an explicit spec for an assistant-UAT remote request builder that can generate the `remote-run-request.json` and `remote-test-request.json` pair from bounded scenario inputs.
The proposed helper preserves the existing remote roundtrip control plane and the existing imported `assistant-uat` evidence contract.
completion_signals: A factory-root spec exists for the assistant-UAT request builder and includes evidence from the current handcrafted Codex personal assistant remote requests plus the implemented builder surfaces.
PackFactory can generate the canonical `remote-run-request.json` and `remote-test-request.json` pair plus an auditable scenario manifest from one bounded assistant-UAT scenario input.
escalation_conditions: The helper design would bypass the PackFactory remote roundtrip control plane or invent a separate remote execution path.
The helper would require broad new test suites instead of reusing current bounded validation and roundtrip surfaces.
tasks.summary: Design a factory-native assistant-UAT remote request builder so PackFactory can generate bounded remote Codex acceptance runs without handcrafted runner payloads.
